name: Gujarat
landmark: Ahmedabad UNESCO Old City, Gir's Asiatic lions, and the Statue of Unity
stopoverNote: Ahmedabad is the best hub. The Kevadia→Gir and Dwarka→Ahmedabad legs are long; use the Kevadia evening train back to Ahmedabad plus an overnight bus/train toward Sasan Gir, and consider exiting Dwarka via Rajkot airport. Kutch/Rann of Kutch is a separate Nov–Feb winter add-on, not a year-round stop.

diet.vegetarian: Gujarat is overwhelmingly vegetarian and one of India's easiest states for pure-veg travel. Gujarati thali, dhokla, fafda-jalebi, khakhra, thepla, undhiyu and Jain food are widely available; Gujarat is also a dry state, so alcohol is prohibited without a tourist permit.
diet.vegan: Many Gujarati dishes use ghee, curd or milk, so ask clearly for food made without dairy. Fafda, khakhra, plain thepla, some farsan and oil-cooked undhiyu can be vegan when prepared without ghee; Ahmedabad and Vadodara have the best vegan-labelled options.
phrases: Bina ghee, bina dahi, bina dudh = without ghee, curd or milk
Sirf tel mein banao = cook only in oil
Jain khana milega? = is Jain food available?

cities.name: Ahmedabad
cities.notes: India's first UNESCO World Heritage City — 600-year-old pol lanes, Sabarmati Ashram, stepwells and one of India's great vegetarian street-food cultures.

cities.name: Statue of Unity / Kevadia
cities.notes: Ekta Nagar gateway to the world's tallest statue, Sardar Sarovar Dam viewpoints, the Narmada valley, Valley of Flowers and family-friendly ticketed attractions.

cities.name: Gir / Sasan Gir
cities.notes: The last wild home of the Asiatic lion. Core-zone jeep safaris must be pre-booked on the official portal and the park closes for monsoon from mid-June to mid-October.

cities.name: Somnath
cities.notes: Arabian Sea pilgrimage town anchored by Somnath Temple, the first Jyotirlinga, with coastal ghats, mythology sites and an evening light-and-sound show.

cities.name: Dwarka
cities.notes: Char Dham pilgrimage city of Lord Krishna at the Arabian Sea and Gulf of Kutch, with Dwarkadhish Temple, Jyotirlinga shrines and Bet Dwarka island ferries.

itinerary.sim: Any Indian prepaid SIM (Jio or Airtel) works well in Ahmedabad, Kevadia, Somnath and Dwarka; Gir forest interiors can be patchy. Buy at Ahmedabad airport/store with ID.

Ahmedabad.name: Ahmedabad
Ahmedabad.note: Two days lets you split the UNESCO walled city from Gandhi sites, Adalaj Stepwell and riverfront time. Start heritage walks before 8am in hot months.

days.note: Old-city lanes are clustered but uneven; start cool and early, then return for Manek Chowk's night-food scene if energy allows.

activities.name: AMC Morning Heritage Walk from Kalupur Swaminarayan Temple
activities.tip: Starts around 7:45am; book ahead through the municipal heritage site for pol lanes, havelis, Manek Chowk and mosque stops.

activities.name: Jama Masjid, Bhadra Fort and Teen Darwaza
activities.cost: Jama Masjid free; Bhadra Fort nominal ₹15–₹25
activities.tip: The 15th-century mosque and gateway sit close together; dress modestly and expect busy lanes.

activities.name: Sidi Saiyyed Mosque jali
activities.tip: Morning light is best for the famous stone tree-of-life lattice.

activities.name: Manek Chowk street food crawl
activities.tip: Jewellers' market by day, vegetarian street-food zone by night; try khichu, sandwiches, kulfi and fafda-jalebi in the morning.

hotels.name: Budget hotel near Ellisbridge or Ashram Road

days.note: Adalaj is ~20km north; hire a cab/auto for the stepwell, then keep the evening flexible for Kankaria or the riverfront.

activities.name: Sabarmati Ashram and Hridaya Kunj
activities.tip: Open from morning to evening; the museum is strongest early before tour groups arrive.

activities.name: Adalaj Stepwell (Vav)
activities.tip: The 5-storey Indo-Islamic stepwell is most photogenic around 9–11am when shafts of light enter.

activities.name: Kankaria Lake and zoo
activities.tip: Closed Mondays; best for families, toy train and lake circuit walks.

activities.name: Sabarmati Riverfront evening walk
activities.tip: Lively around 6–8pm with food vendors; skip if Kankaria runs long.

meals: Sev tameta and undhiyu in winter

Statue of Unity / Kevadia.name: Statue of Unity / Kevadia
Statue of Unity / Kevadia.note: A long but efficient day trip from Ahmedabad by Jan Shatabdi train, or an overnight if you want slower family pacing around Ekta Nagar.

days.theme: World's tallest statue and Narmada valley
days.note: Book Statue of Unity viewing-gallery tickets online; standard queues can stretch on weekends. The full complex takes 5–7 hours.

activities.name: Statue of Unity + museum + 153m viewing gallery
activities.tip: Buy online at soutickets.in; the gallery views over Sardar Sarovar Dam and the Narmada gorge are the headline.

activities.name: Valley of Flowers
activities.tip: Best bloom and colour from November to February, but pleasant for a short walk most dry-season days.

activities.name: Ekta Cruise / boat tour
activities.tip: Choose afternoon light for statue views from the river.

activities.name: Kevadia Jungle Safari
activities.tip: A small wildlife zone; skip if you are heading to Gir and time is tight.

meals: Food court inside Statue of Unity complex
Simple Gujarati thali in Ekta Nagar

Gir / Sasan Gir.name: Gir / Sasan Gir
Gir / Sasan Gir.note: Pre-book Gir Jungle Trail permits on the official portal up to 90 days ahead; the park closes mid-June to mid-October for monsoon, so replace Gir in that window.

days.theme: Asiatic lions at dawn and dusk
days.note: The safari day starts before sunrise; book both dawn and afternoon slots if lions are the trip priority, because sightings are likely but never guaranteed.

activities.name: Dawn jeep safari — Gir Jungle Trail core zone
activities.tip: Official booking only at girlion.gujarat.gov.in; carry Aadhaar/passport, stay inside the jeep and bring binoculars/zoom lens.

activities.name: Dusk jeep safari — core zone
activities.tip: Golden-hour photography is better and lions often move near water in the afternoon heat.

activities.name: Kamleshwar Dam crocodile and bird stop
activities.tip: Use the midday gap between safaris for marsh crocodiles and scenic water views near Sasan.

days.theme: Devaliya interpretation zone and onward to Somnath
days.note: A flexible second morning improves wildlife odds without delaying the easy 70km afternoon transfer to Somnath.

activities.name: Devaliya Safari Park interpretation zone
activities.tip: A smaller fenced area with near-guaranteed lion sightings; useful if the core safari was quiet or for families.

activities.name: Gir Crocodile Breeding Centre
activities.tip: Short conservation stop near Sasan village, especially good with kids.

activities.name: Maldhari community interaction
activities.cost: Free or guided resort tour ₹200–₹400 pp
activities.tip: Go only with a responsible local guide and avoid intrusive photography.

meals: Packed snacks for the Gir→Somnath road

Somnath.name: Somnath
Somnath.note: A relaxed coastal pilgrimage day: visit the temple at quiet dawn, return for evening aarti, then stay for the sea-facing light-and-sound show.

days.note: All major Somnath stops sit within 5–10km. Temple security is strict: modest dress and no photography inside the sanctum.

activities.name: Somnath Temple darshan + Sandhya Aarti
activities.tip: Go at 6am for quiet darshan and return around 7pm for the moving sea-facing aarti.

activities.name: Somnath Light & Sound Show
activities.tip: Buy tickets as soon as you arrive; the 8pm show narrates temple history on the rear wall by the sea.

activities.name: Bhalka Tirth
activities.tip: A peaceful Krishna shrine 5km away; auto return is usually ₹50–₹80.

activities.name: Triveni Sangam Ghat and Somnath Beach
activities.tip: Good sunset walk; beach camel rides usually cost ₹50–₹100.

activities.name: Prabhas Patan Museum
activities.tip: Best for history fans interested in old Somnath sculptures and Prabhasa Kshetra finds.

meals: Tea and snacks near the beach promenade

Dwarka.name: Dwarka
Dwarka.note: Two days keeps temple timing sane: Dwarkadhish and nearby shrines first, then Bet Dwarka by ferry before a long return toward Ahmedabad or Rajkot airport.

days.theme: Char Dham darshan and Jyotirlinga circuit
days.note: Dwarkadhish Temple closes in the afternoon, so plan morning darshan, a lunch break, then evening aarti and sunset by the Gomti.

activities.name: Dwarkadhish Temple darshan + Sandhya Aarti
activities.tip: Mangala Aarti around 6:30am and Sandhya Aarti around 7:15pm are most atmospheric; no leather items in the sanctum.

activities.name: Rukmini Devi Temple
activities.tip: A 12th-century temple 2–3km from town; auto return usually ₹50–₹100.

activities.name: Nageshwar Jyotirlinga Temple
activities.tip: 16km from Dwarka with a giant Shiva statue; hire auto/cab for ₹250–₹400 return.

activities.name: Gomti Ghat and Dwarka Lighthouse sunset
activities.tip: Walk the riverfront after temple hours for sea breeze and sunset views.

days.note: Bet Dwarka ferries leave from Okha, 30km away. Start early, return by afternoon, then choose overnight train/bus or road transfer to Rajkot airport.

activities.name: Bet Dwarka Island via Okha ferry
activities.tip: Ferries usually run 6am–7pm every 15–20 minutes; monsoon seas can disrupt crossings.

activities.name: Hanuman Dandi and Bet Dwarka shoreline
activities.tip: Combine with the island temple visit before returning to Okha.

activities.name: Shankhodhhar conch-shell vendor street
activities.tip: Browse marine handicrafts but avoid buying protected shells or coral.

meals: Packed snacks for Okha ferry morning

connections.method: Jan Shatabdi train to Ekta Nagar, GSRTC bus or private cab
Evening train back to Ahmedabad, then overnight bus/train to Junagadh or Sasan Gir; direct car only if starting very early
Private taxi, GSRTC bus via Veraval, or shared jeep/auto
Private car via Porbandar, GSRTC bus, or Veraval→Dwarka train

extras: Gir lion safari is the highest-feasibility risk: permits should be pre-booked at girlion.gujarat.gov.in, carry original ID, and the park closes every year from mid-June to mid-October for monsoon.
Gujarat is a dry state under prohibition law. Tourists need a temporary alcohol permit from licensed hotels; most travellers simply plan an alcohol-free trip.

Rann of Kutch / white desert is not part of this main route because it is far northwest of Saurashtra and is honestly a Nov–Feb / Rann Utsav winter-only experience; outside the season the salt flats can be wet or inaccessible.
Dwarka→Ahmedabad is a long 440km return: overnight train/bus is cheapest, but time-pressed travellers should transfer 170km to Rajkot and fly onward.
If driving Somnath→Dwarka, stop at Porbandar for Kirti Mandir, Gandhi's birthplace, and optionally Madhavpur beach.
Quoted fees are verified or cross-checked for late 2025–Jul 2026 where available; reconfirm government tickets, ferry timings and temple show schedules on the day.
